perturbation.original_text: The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.changed_text: The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. Deductions will be made from the gross fees paid to mentor in compliance with applicable tax laws and regulations. The Mentor is responsible for any additional deductions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'No deductions' will be made from the mentor's fees. The modification states that 'Deductions will be made', contradicting the original statement. This introduces ambiguity regarding the mentor's compensation and potential tax liabilities.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Income Tax Law (EStG) mandates the deduction of income tax from payments for services rendered by individuals. This change removes the explicit negation of tax deductions, aligning the contract more closely with statutory tax requirements, but contradicting the original clause. The original text promised no deductions, while in reality tax deductions are required by law.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'No deductions will be made', which directly contradicts the modified text stating 'Deductions will be made'. The Law Explanation suggests this change aligns the contract with tax law, implying the original text was non-compliant. Although the provided law snippets do not contain the explicit law, the 'Perturbation Explanation' and 'Law Explanation' suggest the change introduces ambiguity regarding the mentor's compensation and tax liabilities, and that the original text promised no deductions, while in reality tax deductions are required by law, creating a direct contradiction and rendering the original contract language unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Term This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may not be terminated except for important cause. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement , any Confidential Information must be kept confidential for as long as such Confidential Information is not publicly known unless it becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor. This agreement may not be changed or modified, except by an agreement in writing , signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Term This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may be terminated at any time by either party with thirty (30) days written notice. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement , any Confidential Information must be kept confidential for as long as such Confidential Information is not publicly known unless it becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor. This agreement may not be changed or modified, except by an agreement in writing , signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that it 'may not be terminated except for important cause.' The modification states that it 'may be terminated at any time by either party with thirty (30) days written notice.' This directly contradicts the original statement, creating uncertainty about the agreement's termination conditions.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Civil Code (BGB) § 314 dictates that long-term contracts can only be terminated for 'important cause' (wichtiger Grund), especially if the contract is essential for one party. The modification allows for termination 'at any time' with 30 days notice, which may contradict the protection against arbitrary termination provided under German law for contracts of this nature, especially if the mentoring role is crucial for the participant's business development.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 314 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 314 Kündigung von Dauerschuldverhältnissen aus wichtigem Grund (1) Dauerschuldverhältnisse kann jeder Vertragsteil aus wichtigem Grund ohne Einhaltung einer Kündigungsfrist kündigen. Ein wichtiger Grund liegt vor, wenn dem kündigenden Teil unter Berücksichtigung aller Umstände des Einzelfalls und unter Abwägung der beiderseitigen Interessen die Fortsetzung des Vertragsverhältnisses bis zur vereinbarten Beendigung oder bis zum Ablauf einer Kündigungsfrist nicht zugemutet werden kann. (2) Besteht der wichtige Grund in der Verletzung einer Pflicht aus dem Vertrag, ist die Kündigung erst nach erfolglosem Ablauf einer zur Abhilfe bestimmten Frist oder nach erfolgloser Abmahnung zulässig. Für die Entbehrlichkeit der Bestimmung einer Frist zur Abhilfe und für die Entbehrlichkeit einer Abmahnung findet § 323 Absatz 2 Nummer 1 und 2 entsprechende Anwendung. Die Bestimmung einer Frist zur Abhilfe und eine Abmahnung sind auch entbehrlich, wenn besondere Umstände vorliegen, die unter Abwägung der beiderseitigen Interessen die sofortige Kündigung rechtfertigen. (3) Der Berechtigte kann nur innerhalb einer angemessenen Frist kündigen, nachdem er vom Kündigungsgrund Kenntnis erlangt hat. (4) Die Berechtigung, Schadensersatz zu verlangen, wird durch die Kündigung nicht ausgeschlossen. Fußnote (+++ § 314: Zur Nichtanwendung vgl. § 10 Abs. 5 KredWG +++) zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The original contract stipulates termination only for 'important cause,' while the modified version allows termination 'at any time by either party with thirty (30) days written notice.' This directly contradicts the original termination condition, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation. The Law Explanation points to § 314 of the German Civil Code (BGB), which requires 'important cause' for terminating long-term contracts. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 both explicitly state '§ 314 Kündigung von Dauerschuldverhältnissen aus wichtigem Grund' (Termination of long-term contractual relationships for important cause), reinforcing the legal requirement that long-term contracts can only be terminated for an important reason. The modification directly removes this requirement, allowing for arbitrary termination with notice, which undermines the protections afforded under German law, creating a strong legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Mentor shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party or to Mentor ’s employees and/or employer without the prior written consent o f the Participants. Mentor shall require his/her 5 employees who will have access to Confidential Information to commit to a non -disclosure agreement that protects the Confidential Information to at least the same degree as this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent o f the Participants. Mentor's employees are authorized to be informed of Confidential Information necessary for them to fulfil their duties. Mentor shall require his/her 5 employees who will have access to Confidential Information to commit to a non -disclosure agreement that protects the Confidential Information to at least the same degree as this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prevents disclosure to the Mentor's employees or employer, but requires a NDA for employees with access. The modification now allows disclosure to the Mentor's employees, with the caveat that the employees need the information to fulfill their duties, but still requires NDAs. This creates internal conflict on how/when information is disseminated.
perturbation.law_explanation: The German Trade Secrets Act (GeschGehG) § 4 requires that trade secrets are subject to reasonable steps to maintain secrecy. By allowing disclosure to employees without specifying what 'necessary for them to fulfil their duties' means, and previously forbidding it, the contract introduces vagueness. This could weaken the 'reasonable steps' argument and make it more difficult to enforce trade secret protection, especially if an employee later discloses the information improperly.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 4 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 4 Handlungsverbote (1) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht erlangt werden durch 1. unbefugten Zugang zu, unbefugte Aneignung oder unbefugtes Kopieren von Dokumenten, Gegenständen, Materialien, Stoffen oder elektronischen Dateien, die der rechtmäßigen Kontrolle des Inhabers des Geschäftsgeheimnisses unterliegen und die das Geschäftsgeheimnis enthalten oder aus denen sich das Geschäftsgeheimnis ableiten lässt, oder 2. jedes sonstige Verhalten, das unter den jeweiligen Umständen nicht dem Grundsatz von Treu und Glauben unter Berücksichtigung der anständigen Marktgepflogenheit entspricht. (2) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht nutzen oder offenlegen, wer 1. das Geschäftsgeheimnis durch eine eigene Handlung nach Absatz 1 a) Nummer 1 oder b) Nummer 2 erlangt hat, 2. gegen eine Verpflichtung zur Beschränkung der Nutzung des Geschäftsgeheimnisses verstößt oder 3. gegen eine Verpflichtung verstößt, das Geschäftsgeheimnis nicht offenzulegen. (3) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht erlangen, nutzen oder offenlegen, wer das Geschäftsgeheimnis über eine andere Person erlangt hat und zum Zeitpunkt der Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung weiß oder wissen müsste, dass diese das Geschäftsgeheimnis entgegen Absatz 2 genutzt oder offengelegt hat. Das gilt insbesondere, wenn die Nutzung in der Herstellung, dem Anbieten, dem Inverkehrbringen oder der Einfuhr, der Ausfuhr oder der Lagerung für diese Zwecke von rechtsverletzenden Produkten besteht. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction by creating ambiguity around the handling of confidential information, potentially weakening trade secret protection under German law. The original text strictly prohibited disclosure to Mentor's employees without prior written consent. The changed text permits disclosure to employees if 'necessary for them to fulfill their duties'. This introduces vagueness that could undermine 'reasonable steps to maintain secrecy' as required by § 4 of the GeschGehG, especially if an employee later discloses the information improperly. While the NDA requirement for employees remains, the allowed disclosure introduces a level of risk not present in the original contract, potentially jeopardizing trade secret protection.
